title: Strait of Hormuz 'open', but how long till transit resumes? The explosive mine challenge
content: A tentative US-Iran deal signals an end to a 100-day conflict impacting global oil. While prices dipped, analysts warn full recovery of Strait of Hormuz flows will take weeks, possibly months, due to shipping bottlenecks and ongoing mine clearance. Producers await lasting peace assurances before fully resuming output, with full normalization expected gradually.

title: 'Operation Tiger' in final stage: 7 MPs from Uddhav camp may switch to Shinde faction
content: Shiv Sena leader Krupal Tumane claims seven Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs are in final stages of joining Eknath Shinde's faction under 'Operation Tiger'. This move, expected before the Monsoon Session, would bolster the NDA's Lok Sabha numbers. Thackeray's party, however, dismisses these claims, asserting unity.

title: ‘Drugged, raped, filmed’: Nagpur woman alleges abuse, 'hypnosis’ during forced conversion
content: A video surfaces showing a woman pleading to be released while a man chants religious verses, allegedly linked to her claims of rape, blackmail, and forced religious conversion in Nagpur. The woman alleges her former acquaintance, Ayyaz Taj Madare, subjected her to rituals, spiked her drink, and extorted money before forcing a conversion and marriage.

title: Iran goal-scorer Mohebi's 'gun' celebration angers fans, sparks calls for FIFA WC ban
content: Iran's Mohammad Mohebi sparked online debate after scoring a crucial equalizer against New Zealand in their World Cup qualifier. His goal celebration, a hand gesture resembling a gun, drew mixed reactions amid pre-match political protests. While some deemed it inappropriate, others defended it as an emotional, sporting reaction.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: First to Leave, Never Far Behind: Kane Williamson and the Fab Four
content: For years, the Fab Four debate searched endlessly for a winner. As Kane Williamson becomes the first member of the quartet to retire, this is not a ranking but a reflection. Amid Kohli's intensity, Smith's absurdity and Root's volume, Williamson quietly went about his business. From match-winning runs and captaincy highs to carrying the heaviest individual load, the New Zealander built a career defined by greatness without the noise.

title: As drone warfare evolves, army air defence seeks to strengthen new types of aerial targets
content: India's Army Air Defence is enhancing its training with three new aerial target systems to counter evolving drone threats. These include rocket-propelled targets for high-speed missile simulation, swarm drone replicators for multi-target engagement, and multirotor copters mimicking slow-moving helicopters. This procurement aims to bolster preparedness against a wide array of modern aerial adversaries.

title: US senator says Tesla benefited from govt support, Elon Musk replies
content: Elon Musk has refuted claims that his companies, Tesla and SpaceX, owe their success to government aid, stating such incentives represent less than 2% of their value. The debate ignited between Senators Mike Lee and Ed Markey over taxpayer contributions to Musk's wealth. Musk asserted that removing EV tax credits actually boosted Tesla's market share.

title: 'Get on a plane and leave': Iran coach explodes after shock order following WC match
content: Iran drew their World Cup opener 2-2 with New Zealand but faced immediate travel disruptions. Coach Amir Ghalenoei expressed frustration, stating the team was forced to leave the US for Mexico post-match, hindering recovery. Captain Mehdi Taremi also highlighted visa issues for delegation members, calling for FIFA's assistance amidst ongoing logistical challenges.

title: Why India's 'Tomahawk' could be as important as BrahMos in future conflicts
content: Brahmos missiles swiftly neutralized Pakistan's air power on May 10, 2025, demonstrating decisive capability. However, prolonged modern conflicts necessitate a cost-effective solution. India is exploring the Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile (LR-LACM), a cheaper, 1,500 km range weapon, to complement Brahmos and enhance sustained combat operations, ensuring greater strategic staying power.

title: Telegram banned in India till June 22, but govt wants one more feature disabled
content: The government has temporarily blocked Telegram access until June 22 to combat cheating rackets defrauding students ahead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination. The National Testing Agency also mandated disabling Telegram's message-editing feature to prevent the creation of fake "paper leak" evidence, aiming to ensure a secure re-test.

title: Tum Pukar Lo: Hemant Kumar — the voice that turned silence into song
content: Hemant Kumar's voice and music left an indelible mark on Hindi cinema. His compositions, often built from silence and emotional subtlety, created timeless melodies. Collaborations with Lata Mangeshkar produced iconic songs. As a composer, his work on films like Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam showcased his mastery.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ executive producer James Barker passes away at 40
content: Executive producer of 'Love Island USA,' James Barker, has passed away at the age of 40 while filming the latest season in Fiji. ITV America and Peacock confirmed his unexpected passing due to a medical emergency. Barker was a highly valued member of the production team, credited with revolutionizing the show.

title: Karan Johar backs 'Odiyan'; Prithviraj, Manju Warrier lead folklore mystery
content: Karan Johar officially announces his entry into Malayalam cinema with 'Odiyan,' starring Prithviraj Sukumaran and Manju Warrier. Directed by Rahul Sadasivan, the film blends folklore and mystery, promising a unique pan-Indian release across five languages. Johar expressed excitement for this collaboration, highlighting Sadasivan's skill in native ghost storytelling.

title: US-Iran peace deal: What opening of Hormuz would mean for India’s crude oil supplies
content: Prior to the outbreak of the conflict, the Gulf region supplied roughly 40% of India's crude oil imports. After the war began on February 28, inflows from the region declined sharply.

title: 'Increase in crimes involving North Indians': Annamalai demands stricter checks on migrants
content: Former BJP leader K Annamalai voiced concerns over alleged increased involvement of North Indian individuals in crimes across Tamil Nadu, following a child's death. He questioned the state government's mechanisms for tracking migrant workers and urged for a comprehensive database to enhance oversight and prevent future incidents.

title: ‘This could save lives’: Doctors laud Centre's tough stance on cough syrups
content: A new regulation from the Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare now requires a doctor's prescription for purchasing cough syrups and other syrup medicines. This change, effective immediately, removes these medications from over-the-counter availability. The amendment to the Drugs Rules, 1945, was finalized after considering public and stakeholder feedback.

title: FIFA WC standings: How groups shape up after 16 matches & historic day of draws
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is heating up with a historic day of four draws, a feat unseen since 1958. Debutants Cape Verde held Spain, while Belgium, Uruguay, and Iran also drew their opening matches. With heavyweights yet to play, the battle for the knockout stage is intensifying, proving no team can be underestimated.

title: Maps miss the runway, route blooper greets passengers at Noida International Airport
content: On its first day, Noida airport grappled with substantial navigational quirks, as travelers encountered misdirections from outdated GPS systems. Passengers were mistakenly routed to the cargo entrance by Google Maps, resulting in irritation, unexpected expenses, and delays. In response, the airport authorities are collaborating with Google to refine the traveler access pathways for a seamless experience ahead.

title: IND A vs SL A: Why was Super Over played despite fading light? Decision raises eyebrows
content: A tied India A vs Sri Lanka A match ended in controversy after a Super Over was played in near-darkness. Despite deteriorating light, captain Tilak Varma pushed for a result, leading to player struggles and an ugly post-match altercation. Officials' decision to continue under poor visibility remains a major talking point.

title: 'Hands folded, head down': TMC's 'Pushpa' Jahangir Khan paraded again in Falta - video
content: Diamond Harbour Police have reopened a six-year-old case involving an attack on BJP leader JP Nadda. The re-investigation aims to address flaws in the initial probe, which allegedly saw key accused, including arrested Trinamool leader Jahangir Khan, discharged. Khan is already facing multiple charges and was recently paraded through streets to reconstruct the crime scene.

title: '100% with development': Mamata aide praises Suvendu amid TMC rebellion
content: Amidst a significant rebellion within the Trinamool Congress, Mamata Banerjee's close aide Firhad Hakim has signaled a potential shift in approach towards the new BJP government. Hakim expressed optimism for "100% development" in Bengal under Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, emphasizing the need to prioritize progress over party politics and suggesting a willingness to collaborate for the state's advancement.

title: Oil's not well? US emergency stockpile falls to lowest level
content: The US emergency oil stockpile has hit its lowest point since 1983, with the Trump administration drawing down reserves to mitigate economic impacts from global conflicts. Recent releases have pushed the Strategic Petroleum Reserve to less than half full, raising concerns about operational capacity and vulnerability to disruptions like hurricanes.

title: Gold price prediction: Will gold prices rise from current levels? Check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices may rise in the near-term as oil prices ease but the US Federal Reserve’s meeting is an important thing to watch, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Smriti Mandhana lone Indian in TIME's list of 100 most influential people in sports
content: India's cricket star Smriti Mandhana has been recognized by TIME magazine on its 100 Most Influential People in Sports 2026 list. The Mumbai-born opener is the sole Indian to feature, celebrated for her record-breaking batting prowess and leadership in team triumphs, including the Women's Premier League and the ICC Women's ODI World Cup.

title: 'Forced to chant Urdu, eat meat': Woman alleges rape and religious conversion in Nagpur
content: A married woman in Nagpur has accused her former acquaintance and his associates of rape, blackmail, and forced religious conversion. She alleges she was drugged, photographed in compromising positions, and extorted. The accused reportedly performed conversion rituals, declared her married to one of them, and forced her to eat meat before further assaults.

title: Delhi techie found dead in Mussoorie; husband's night-out claim under scrutiny
content: A 27-year-old software engineer from Delhi was discovered deceased under perplexing circumstances at a Mussoorie homestay on Monday. She had arrived with her husband on Saturday night. Police found her naked on the floor with bloodstains and two empty liquor bottles in the room. The husband claims they drank before sleeping and he found her unconscious upon waking.

title: B-52 bomber crash in California: How it became a constant in US wars from Vietnam to Middle East
content: A US Air Force B-52 bomber crashed during a test mission at Edwards Air Force Base, killing all eight crew members. This iconic aircraft, in service since 1955, remains a vital part of US military operations, from the Cold War to recent conflicts. Despite its age, the B-52 is undergoing modernization to ensure its relevance for decades to come.

title: 'Egg of the Sun': Rs 1 lakh-a-piece Miyazaki mango offered to Lord Ram in Ayodhya
content: The world's most expensive Japanese Miyazaki mango, known as the "Egg of the Sun," was offered to Lord Ram at Ayodhya's Ram Mandir. Cultivated locally by farmer Omprakash Singh, this rare fruit, valued at up to Rs 3 lakh per kilogram, was presented as the season's first ripe offering to the deity.

title: Watch: Lebanese journalist attacked by Israeli drones while reporting
content: A journalist, Hadi Hoteit, was injured by Israeli drone shrapnel while reporting in Kfar Tebnit, southern Lebanon. Despite clear press markings on his vest, helmet, and car, he was targeted in an open area.

title: 'Attackers belonged to RSS': CJP founder says 'not scared' after Jaipur protest assault
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke, attacked during a Jaipur protest, remains undeterred, alleging RSS links to the assault. He vows to continue peaceful protests demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET paper leak, asserting that such attacks won't deter their 'satyagraha' for student justice.

title: 'We didn’t manage the visa money in time': Vozinha breaks down over mum’s absence
content: Cape Verde's veteran goalkeeper Vozinha, 40, was overcome with emotion after a stellar Player of the Match performance against Spain, a 0-0 World Cup draw. Tears flowed not just from the historic result, but for his late grandparents and a mother unable to attend due to visa issues.  His seven saves secured a memorable debut for his nation.

title: 'Even if you slit my throat...': Abhishek defiant after 11 hours ED questioning
content: Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee asserted he wouldn't be intimidated, even after an 11-hour ED questioning regarding West Bengal's teacher recruitment scam. He stated his full cooperation and commitment to appear if summoned again, alleging attempts to weaken opposition parties. The ED probe investigates alleged money laundering linked to the scam.

title: Will Sooryavanshi face ICC action? What the Code of Conduct says after pushing SL player
content: Teenage batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faces potential sanctions after a heated post-match altercation with a Sri Lanka A player. The 15-year-old was involved in a physical exchange following India's Super Over defeat. The incident could lead to fines or demerit points under the ICC Code of Conduct, with the match referee to decide on sanctions.

title: Are brokers and real estate agents covered under RERA Act? Here’s what the law says
content: Navigating the property market? The Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016, mandates registration for agents dealing with registered projects. This ensures transparency and accountability, requiring agents to obtain a registration number from their state's RERA authority. Failure to comply can lead to rejection or revocation of registration, safeguarding buyers.

title: Is Form 16 mandatory for ITR filing & what to do if employer has not issued it?
content: Ideally, you should also cross check information across all your major documents such as Form 16, Form 26AS, Annual Information Statement (AIS), interest statements etc. to see if there are any discrepancies.

title: UPSC prelims result: 13,343 candidates qualify for Mains; check next steps
content: UPSC has released the Civil Services Prelims 2026 result in PDF format, allowing candidates to download the roll number list and check their qualification status for the Mains examination. A total of 13,343 candidates have cleared the Preliminary stage against 1,016 vacancies. Qualified candidates must complete mandatory online formalities between June 19 and June 28, 2026. The Civil Services Main Examination will begin on August 21, 2026, marking the next stage of selection.

title: Kerala plans ‘outbreak calendar’ to boost disease prediction and response
content: Health Minister K. Muraleedharan blames poor monsoon cleaning for current Shigella outbreak in Kerala

title: Lok Sabha Speaker invites Mamata faction to ‘present case’ amid TMC split
content: Mamata Banerjee faction claims that the invitation was sent at 2 p.m. on June 15 asking party General Secretary Abhishek Banerjee to meet the Speaker by 4 p.m. the same day, while he was being interrogated by the Enforcement Directorate in Kolkata

title: Tribal body wants ‘derogatory’ words removed from Ram Charan’s ‘Peddi’
content: The Vizianagaram body said it had petitioned the Superintendent of Police, citing the SC and ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act

title: We are the largest parliamentary force from West Bengal: NCPI in a social media post
content: 20 rebel MPs of Trinamool Congress have announced their decision to merge with NCPI after they met  the Speaker of Lok Sabha Om Birla in Delhi on Sunday and submitted a letter to that effect

title: Navi Mumbai, Guwahati airports among world’s ‘most beautiful’
content: Adani Airport Holdings Limited says Prix Versailles, one of the leading architecture and design awards, recognised the two airports

title: Kakoli seeks Kalyan’s expulsion from Lok Sabha, writes to Speaker
content: Rebel Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar wrote to Speaker Om Birla and cited repeated verbal abuse and misogynistic conduct during House proceedings allegedly by Kalyan Banerjee

title: Supreme Court seeks responses of Centre, States on plea for use of Aadhaar as identity proof only
content: The petition contended that the existing verification mechanism under Form-6 is inadequate and might allow individuals without proper supporting documents to be included in the electoral database

title: Perundurai bypoll: First-level checks of EVMs begin
content: The bypoll was necessitated after S. Jayakumar of the AIADMK resigned his seat and joined the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) within two weeks of being elected.

title: Kerala’s revised Budget for 2026-27 likely to give thrust to higher education sector
content: Budget expected to provide roadmap for UDF’s ‘Indira Guarantee’ of providing monthly stipend of ₹1,000 to college-going girls

title: When will Tamil Nadu CM Vijay break his silence, asks DMK leader Stalin
content: He wondered whether the Chief Minister expected him to remain silent for six months even when the safety and lives of children had become a matter of concern.

title: MAATI helps women artists from Bihar to look beyond art
content: MAATI, short for Mithila Art Artisan Transformative Initiative, works with women artisans from Madhubhani and Darbhanga in Bihar. Four women artisans showcased their works in Bengaluru, ranging from textile to wall art

title: Korikana Ravikumar appointed to Jana Sena committee coordinating inductions in State
content: The Srikakulam leader said a clean image would be the top priority in screening new entrants ahead of the local body and 2029 polls

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay inaugurates two-day training programme for MLAs
content: Former Assembly Secretaries and officials will train the MLAs on the Rules of Procedure of the Assembly and on the functioning of specific government departments. Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar said 146 MLAs were first-time legislators and several of them were youngsters.

title: Kerala BJP councillor R. Sreelekha accuses makers of ‘Dridam’ and ‘Bhoothakalam’ of plagiarising her stories
content: In a Facebook post, she alleges that the core idea of films ‘Dridam’ and ‘Bhoothakalam’ are lifted from stories and videos she had shared on her social media timeline in the past

title: Yoga session at Vizag golf club blends fitness with tourism push ahead of Yoga Day
content: MP M. Sribharat and Collector M. Abhishikth Kishore joined the event, the third under the initiative, before the June 21 mass demonstration on Beach Road

title: APSRTC announces free travel for NEET-UG candidates on June 21 examination day
content: Candidates can travel free on showing their hall ticket, with extra buses and help desks at major stations

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Telegram app restricted in India at NTA request
content: The agency said that several Telegram groups were advertising fake exam papers for the upcoming NEET exam and defrauding candidates willing to pay for them

title: Three killed, three injured as car runs over people after hitting multiple vehicles in Bhubaneswar
content: The mishap took place in the Gajapati Nagar area under the Maitree Vihar Police Station limit, on the road connecting Acharya Vihar to Nalco Square in the city, on June 15

title: Kerala woman MLA Fathima Thahiliya warns online media against ‘paparazzi-style’ filming, threatens legal action
content: In a Facebook post, the first-time MLA from Perambra says that while attending public functions was part of her responsibilities as a people’s representative, there has been a growing trend of individuals entering events in the name of media and filming without proper authorisation or identification

title: Karnataka govt. asks RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat to send representatives to explain legal status on registration of organisation
content: Quoting the 2025-2026 report of the Akhil Bharatiya Pratinidhi Sabha, the highest decision-making body of the RSS, Home Minister Priyank Kharge has said that the organisation has significant footprint in Karnataka, with 4,127 daily shakhas, 1,389 weekly milans, and 60 monthly mandalis

title: India seeks rare earth samples from Rosneft-owned Siberian deposit, source says
content: India ‌is keen to study the mineral composition of the deposit before ‌considering ⁠deeper engagement, said the source, who has knowledge of ⁠the matter and spoke on the condition of anonymity as the discussions are confidential

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: IUML enters new era as Fathima Thahiliya becomes first woman MLA
content: While the IUML has long faced criticism for not fielding women in strong or winnable constituencies, Thahiliya’s victory has challenged that perception and marked a clear shift in narrative

title: Government makes doctor’s prescription mandatory to buy all medicinal syrups, including for cough
content: Following the notification, lozenges and pills and tablets for cough would still be available over the counter.

title: SJM writes to U.S. envoy over Indian seafarers' killing, seeks probe, compensation
content: Mahajan alleged that the U.S. action constituted a serious violation of international law governing the seas, armed conflict, and human rights

title: Tamil Nadu government revises cooperative crop loan waiver up to ₹75,000; expands to all farmers
content: Under the revised announcement, a total of 8,33,773 marginal farmers, 5,16,183 small farmers and 93,548 large farmers will receive waivers amounting to ₹3,599.67 crore, ₹1,995.42 crore and ₹337.15 crore, respectively.

title: Warmth of hospitality during Amarnath yatra will defeat hate poltics: Mehbooba Mufti
content: She urged a peaceful people’s initiative to ensure every Yatri feels welcomed, respected, and protected

title: Shoolagiri wetland turns into toxic bed of industrial waste
content: The conscious dump of industrial waste in the wetland abutting a lake contaminates ground water and the waterbody

title: All MPs are with party only: Shiv Sena (UBT) amid “Operation Tiger” buzz
content: State witnessed “Operation Tiger” buzz, speculating MPs from Shiv Sena (UBT) to quit the party and join Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena

title: ‘Agri’ Krishnamurthy rebuts Shanmugam’s criticism against Palaniswami
content: Palaniswami is “not shy” about convening the executive and general council meetings; he would like to first complete the district-wise consultations, says former Minister

title: Jeddah-bound AI Express flight from Kerala’s Kannur turns back due to engine fault
content: Aircraft with over 180 passengers took off at 7.40 am from Kannur airport, but around two hours into the flight, the pilots saw an engine warning light and decided to return, say airport sources

title: Former DMK Minister Geetha Jeevan’s acquittal from 2002 disproportionate assets case challenged in Madras High Court
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan defers orders on a plea to condone a delay of 839 days in preferring a third party revision against the 2022 acquittal

title: TMC's Abhishek Banerjee appears before Bengal CID in case against his 'inflammatory' statements
content: The FIR against Mr. Banerjee over his alleged inflammatory statements was lodged exactly a month ago, based on a complaint filed by social activist Rajib Sarkar at Baguiati Police Station in North 24 Parganas district on May 5, a day after the assembly election results were declared

title: How can CBI probe be ordered into resignation of four MLAs without evidence, asks Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan say the petitioner had not submitted before the court even basic materials to suspect involvement of corrupt practices

title: 100% ethanol fuel approval cheers sugarcane growers in Mysuru
content: Delegates at the South India Farmers’ Convention held in Mysuru in 2006 passed a resolution urging the Centre to promote ethanol production from sugarcane, and allow its use as a fuel

title: ACB raids Deputy Director of Land and Survey Department, begins search at 12 locations
content: The raids are being carried out at Narahari’s residence and other premises associated with him, including Narayanaguda

title: NEET paper leak: Delhi court allows CBI to quiz 3 accused in jail
content: Sources said the CBI will interrogate Waghmare on June 17, Mr. Lokhande the next day, and Mr. Khairnar on June 19

title: Bangladesh PM’s adviser returns from Delhi airport; says he was ‘humiliated’ by immigration
content: Rahman was held for more than two hours before he was cleared to enter Delhi; by that time, he had made up his mind to return

title: CMRL pay-off case: Company MD Sasidharan Kartha’s son, wife appear before ED for questioning
content: ED had earlier asked Veena T., daughter of former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, to appear for questioning on June 17 as part of the probe into the alleged pay-off case involving Exalogic Solutions Private Limited, her now-defunct firm, and CMRL

title: The trust deficit in India-Bangladesh ties
content: Bangladeshi diplomats have pointed out that Dhaka expected less rhetoric on illegal immigration, and more focus on issues such as the renewal of the 1996 Ganga Water Treaty

title: Chandrababu Naidu releases book on his leadership, ‘CBN@361 Degrees’, in Singapore
content: The author said the Chief Minister’s governance and long-term planning could be compared with leaders such as Lee Kuan Yew and Jacinda Ardern

title: Traffic woes persist amid road widening, flyover construction as monsoon affects IIIT junction underpass work
content: Nearly 5 lakh commuters navigate daily congestion as roads are widened along the IIIT Hyderabad and TCS stretches ahead of the next phase of underpass work.

title: Meeting in Yadgir discusses steps to implement directions issued by Karnataka CM
content: The government of Karnataka is planning to organise a Janaspandana programme in Yadgir to hear people’s grievances

title: After criticism, NCERT to restore original 'Dancing Girl' image in school textbook
content: Iconic bronze figurine of Mohenjo-daro has its bare torso covered in the latest NCERT arts textbook for Class 9; unlike the altered image, the Dancing Girl in a Class 6 textbook appears in a form closer to the original bronze sculpture

title: NCERT textbook content shows ‘deep-rooted conspiracy’ to show judiciary as corrupt: Supreme Court
content: Declaring that “heads must roll”, CJI Surya Kant orders “blanket and complete ban” on the Class 8 social science textbook; initiates contempt action, issues show cause to NCERT director, School Education Secretary

title: Are cartoons in NCERT textbooks proper? Supreme Court asks retired judge panel to review
content: The order came after Solicitor General Tushar Mehta raised the issue and said 'textbook is not a place where you use cartoons'

title: INSWAREB proposes partnership with U.S. universities on irradiated construction materials
content: The institute wants access to the Idaho National Laboratory’s test reactor to validate concrete made from fly ash and rice husk ash

title: Noida International Airport starts functioning as first flight from Lucknow lands
content: IndiGo flies 170 farmers from Noida to meet Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath in the State capital; airport to initially handle 12 daily flights; number set to increase to 42 flights a day from July

title: Indian commercial ships should avoid Shinas Anchorage, shipworkers’ union tells Centre
content: The Outer Port Limit area in the Sultanate of Oman has emerged as one of the most sensitive and high risk zones for merchant vessels, particularly vessels with Indian or Indian-interest tankers, the Forward Seamen’s Union of India says

title: UNSC reform would border on failure if only non-permanent category expanded: India
content: India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Harish Parvathaneni was addressing the meeting, with focus on the ‘Elements Paper’, a document that contains points of convergences and divergences of UN member states on reform of the powerful UN body

title: Leaders from various communities seek ministerial berth for Prasad Abbayya in Karnataka cabinet
content: Nonagenarian businessman G.M. Chikkamath and said that he had never supported any political party, but had extended support based on the work done by Mr. Abbayya

title: ZP CEO seeks report on condition of school, college buildings in Kalaburagi
content: Rahul Sankanur chairs review meeting following collapse of ceiling plaster in a school building in the district

title: Australian Girl, 9, On Pak Vacation Killed After Cop 'Mistakenly' Shoots Her
content: A cop, thinking that the robbers were fleeing in the family's vehicle, "mistakenly" fired his gun, resulting in the death of the 9-year-old and injuring her brother and father, police said.

title: At $10, "The Rock" Will "Assist" You Drive Tesla, Fool Safety Cameras
content: Small plastic figurine heads are being placed near the rearview mirror or windshield where they can be detected by the camera that monitors driver attentiveness.

title: K Annamalai, BJP Spar Over "Militarisation" Of Upcoming NEET Retest
content: In May, the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET) - one of the country's most competitive exams for undergraduate medical seats, attracting millions of candidates - was canceled due to an alleged paper leak.

title: World's Costliest Mango Worth Rs 1 Lakh Offered At Ram Temple In Ayodhya
content: The offering was made on Monday to the deity in keeping with local traditions, with the fruit presented along with a Tulsi (holy basil) leaf.

title: Tamil Nadu Streamlines High-Rise Building Permissions To Reduce Delays
content: The decision removes the need for multiple layers of recommendations and final government approval that developers previously had to secure before commencing projects.

title: US At Odds With Allies Over How Easy It Is To Reopen Hormuz
content: According to one G7 official, there are serious difficulties in finding a common position among the group about how to deal with the situation in Iran.

title: Dhaka Summons Indian Envoy After Bangladesh PM's Adviser Screened At Delhi Airport
content: The Bangladeshi leader was travelling to India on a government invitation. He reportedly had a regular Bangladeshi passport with a SAARC visa and was not carrying a diplomatic passport.

title: 6.7 Earthquake Shakes Part Of Indonesia, Causes Scattered Damage
content: The strong shaking sent people fleeing into open areas in and around Palu, a city of about 400,000 people and the capital of Central Sulawesi province.

title: Poll Body Begins Month-Long Electoral Roll Revision In Andhra Pradesh
content: Announcing the launch, Chief Electoral Officer Vivek Yadav said the exercise is being carried out per directions issued by the Election Commission of India under constitutional and legal provisions governing electoral rolls.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Heir Not-So-Apparent: The New Reality Of Indian Politics
content: Trinamool's turmoil is a warning to regional parties that legacy can open the door, but only legitimacy keeps the house from breaking.

title: Palestinian Leader Announces Presidential Election In Early 2027
content: Palestinian president Mahmoud Abbas has announced that presidential elections will be held in early 2027 and legislative elections in November this year, without...

title: Your Smart Doorbell, Tablet May Be Working For Hackers Without You Knowing
content: The malware, known as residential proxy software, can turn a device into a hidden gateway for internet traffic.

title: 'Banaras Connect': PM Modi Visits Varanasi-Inspired Exhibition In Slovakia
content: The exhibition featured paintings and artworks created by Slovak artists who had recently travelled to Varanasi.

title: Attacks On Educational Institutes Surged Over 40% In 2024-2025: Report
content: The report, Education Under Attack 2026, recorded at least 8,566 attacks on education during the two-year period, marking an increase of more than 40 per cent compared with 2022 and 2023.

title: Wife, Lover Kill Karnataka Man In Hospital With Staff's Help For Rs 2 Crore
content: The police arrested nine people, including the victim's wife and her alleged lover for allegedly conspiring to kill Sandeep Manjarg and manipulate evidence.

title: Utah BASE Jump Kills 2, Including Extreme Athlete Who Performed With Madonna
content: A study focused on BASE jumping in Norway, published in a medical journal in 2007, estimated that BASE jumping carried risks of injury or death five to eight times greater than skydiving.

title: Man On Bike Dies After Plunging Into Unlit, Unfenced Pit In Greater Noida
content: The victim's body and motorcycle were discovered eight to nine hours later on Monday morning by local farmers heading to nearby fields.

title: Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar elected NCPI president ahead of TMC rebels merger with party
content: rebels merged with nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) to dodge disqualification provisions under anti-defection law . the breakaway faction of 20 MPs met speaker Om Birla on Sunday to inform him of their merger with the little-known party . sources said the fourth-term MP was elected president on may 30 .
